passes.notes: Rechecked local SRD Making an Attack, Opportunity Attacks, Reaction, and the spell effect text in cleanroom-input/raw/srd-5.2.1; modeled behavior remains limited to the target being unable to make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.
Route and witness names use Reaction, Opportunity Attack, active effect, movement/reaction discovery, and turn-boundary language from the copied cleanroom vocabulary.
Initial SQNT-03C route subject, owner, fill, and route conversion facts were localized in BattleSubjectKind / BattleReducerRouteSubjectFamily / GenericRouteShape / adapter conversion.
Production runtime dispatches on typed active-effect state and route subjects. Authored spell/action names appear only in the cleanroom adapter branch selector, tests, and evidence metadata.
Reviewer found cleanup lifecycle weakness and stale SQNT-03A artifact records; both are addressed in the fixer round.

Replaced BattleSpellActiveEffects.opportunity_attack_denied bool with BattleOpportunityAttackDenialEffect::Inactive/Active/Expired. Admission and attack-hit establish Active, projection and duration expiry require Active, expiry moves Active to Expired, and cleanup requires Expired before moving to Inactive.
Added opportunity_attack_denial_cleanup_requires_turn_boundary_expiry, which rejects cleanup immediately after admission with StaleSubject and keeps the effect Active.

Refreshed SQNT-03C evidence, latest task artifacts, and RUN_LEDGER records so they no longer point at SQNT-03A. The harness result remains recorded as known stale validator hashes rather than a global clean pass.
The lifecycle connascence now lives in one enum and the reducer state-rejection/transition helpers; future cleanup sequencing changes must update compile-visible variants and focused tests.
